mappings.rationale: Core VSI for this section, covering the human skeletal system (B.7), skeleton structure, and musculatory system, with detailed treatment of vertebrate anatomy.
Addresses the physiology of muscle contraction (C.3), skeletal support, and integumentary function including temperature regulation (C.1).
Covers diversity of body coverings, skeletal systems, and musculature across invertebrate phyla (A.1, B.3, C.4) including Annelida, Echinodermata, and Porifera.
Covers embryology of vertebrate skin (A.5), skeleton (B.4.b), and muscular system (C.5.a), including cell differentiation into connective tissues.
Covers teeth as skeletal elements (B.2.a bone, dentine, enamel), their composition, and evolutionary development in vertebrates (B.4).
Addresses mammalian integument including hair and skin glands (A.3.b), skeletal structure, and vertebrate musculature (C.5).
Covers reptilian epidermal scales (A.3.b), skeletal systems, and musculature, illustrating skin variations among vertebrates (A.4).
Addresses invertebrate exoskeletons and cuticular structures (B.2.a, B.3), invertebrate integuments (A.2), and invertebrate muscle systems (C.4).
Relevant to skin derivatives such as feathers, hair, and horns (A.3.b) as products of sexual dimorphism and sexual selection pressures.
Provides the evolutionary framework for understanding the development of vertebrate skin (A.5), skeleton (B.4.b), and musculatory system (C.5.b).
